Functional Functional cookies help perform certain func", "relevance_score": 0.8}, {"title": "TAM, SAM & SOM: How To Calculate TheSizeOf YourMarket| Antler", "url": "https://www.antler.co/blog/tam-sam-som", "snippet": "[Jina Extracted Content] Title: TAM, SAM & SOM: How To Calculate The Size Of Your Market | Antler URL Source: https://www.antler.co/blog/tam-sam-som Markdown Content: Insights In the competitive world of startups, understanding your market isn\u2019t just important\u2014it\u2019s everything. The gap between a game-changing venture and a struggling business often comes down to knowing your Total Addressable Market (TAM), Serviceable Available Market (SAM), and Serviceable Obtainable Market (SOM). Nail these numbers, and you\u2019ll attract investors, seize opportunities, and carve out your place in the market before the competition does. ![Image 1](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68d622fc073c7e5dead1105e/68f08970f399a3048d8752a8_6881894de441973e2c61b5e5_67dcbe44df1f554ca827b786_TAM%252C%2520SAM%2520%2526%2520SOM_.avif) Without a market, a business doesn\u2019t have much of a future. Actually understanding the potential size and [shape of the market](https://www.antler.co/academy/problem-solution-fit) is a critical step in the early stages of developing a business, as it will help to inform [a winning go-to-market strategy](https://www.antler.co/academy/go-to-market-strategy-for-startups) everything from your short and medium-term objectives, product development, right through to [the way you pitch to investors](https://www.antler.co/academy/pre-seed-pitch-deck). Here\u2019s a beautiful example of market research in action, at the genesis of a business. Facebook (now Meta) is almost ubiquitous in modern life. However, it wasn\u2019t always like that. When Facebook was new, the entire social media sphere was effectively untested and unproven. Facebook (then TheFacebook) needed to prove a lot\u2014not least that there was an actual audience for it. Here\u2019s an actual slide from those early days: ![Image 2: The Facebook Pitch Deck Slide](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68d622fc073c7e5dead1105e/68f08971d52c96744958627b_67dcc22665e19432986f166b_6548a2b7ad3faf3fb5581a71_6527c8fff9346497a50556b9_c528a107-a81d-44d1-894e-cf948ef2a969_The%2525252BFacebook%2525252B-%2525252BEarly%2525252BPitch%2525252BDeck.avif) It makes a pretty compelling argument, right? Without Facebook having such a [successful proposition early in its life](https://www.antler.co/academy/startup-value-proposition), it may have never had the opportunity to grow into what it is today, but investors bought into the promise of tapping into the college students market, and the rest is history. How did Facebook arrive at those numbers, and how can you do the same for your startup? Being able to do this for your own startup idea comes down to three acronyms: TAM, SAM & SOM. By understanding these metrics, you can allocate resources effectively to drive smarter decisions in product development, pricing, and go-to-market strategies. ## Introduction to Market Sizing Market sizing is a crucial step in understanding the potential of a business idea. It involves estimating the total addressable market (TAM), serviceable addressable market (SAM), and serviceabl", "relevance_score": 0.8}, {"title": "MarketSizeTAM, SAM, SOM Diagram withTotalAvailable...", "url": "https://www.infodiagram.com/slides/market-sizetam-sam-som-diagram-with-total-available-serviceable-available-obtainable-market-volume/", "snippet": "[Jina Extracted Content] Title: Market SizeTAM, SAM, SOM Diagram with Total Available, Serviceable Available & Obtainable Market Volume URL Source: https://www.infodiagram.com/slides/market-sizetam-sam-som-diagram-with-total-available-serviceable-available-obtainable-market-volume/ Markdown Content: #
